codeReview.summary: The error-format standardization, timeout wrapper, and fetch-path circuit breaker integration are broadly sound. I found one non-blocking bug in the health-summary accounting for cooled-down circuits.

issues.description: Fix cooled-down circuit accounting in getCircuitSummary(). When an OPEN circuit has passed its cooldown, the function executes `open--` before any open count was added, so the `/api/health` summary can report negative `open` values and an inconsistent breakdown until the next probe transitions the source. This misreports circuit-breaker health exactly in the state operators rely on for diagnostics.
issues.suggestedFix: Count cooled-down OPEN circuits as half-open directly (or transition the stored state first) instead of decrementing the open accumulator.

summary: Pass with a non-blocking diagnostics bug. Runtime behavior is sound, but circuit-breaker health summaries miscount sources whose cooldowns have expired.
